title: pulicaria villosa
intro: Pulicaria villosa, also known as fleabane or common fleabane, is a plant species in the sunflower family that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to provide various health benefits, including soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Pulicaria villosa can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its effects. Additionally, the plant's antiseptic properties make it useful for wound care and skin infections, and it has been traditionally used to treat conditions such as eczema and acne.
